success_surface: Row-level filtering with conditional logic and formatting preservation
effective_behaviors: Exploratory analysis before coding to understand sheet structure and data characteristics
Safe file handling with separate input/output paths to preserve source integrity
Defensive programming with explicit None/null value checks before string operations
In-memory filtering with intermediate data structures before writing results
Automatic column width adjustment based on post-filter content length
Comprehensive execution logging with row statistics for validation
Preservation of worksheet metadata (header row, sheet name) during reconstruction
evidence: Code queried wb.sheetnames, ws.max_row, ws.max_column before implementing filter logic
Sampled first 10 rows from column B to verify data format before full processing
Used load_workbook() to preserve formatting, then created new Workbook() for output
Implemented 'if cell_value is not None' check before calling str(cell_value)[0]
Built filtered_rows list in memory before iterating to write to new workbook
Printed 'Original rows', 'Header row', 'Data rows processed', 'Rows kept', 'Rows deleted' summary

memory_items.title: Pre-process Data Exploration
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect sheet names, dimensions, and sample data before implementing transformation logic.
memory_items.generalization: Query structural properties and validate data formats programmatically before applying complex operations to avoid silent failures.

memory_items.title: Output File Isolation
memory_items.lesson: Never modify the source file directly; always write filtered results to a separate output file.
memory_items.generalization: Separate read and write operations across distinct files to prevent accidental data loss and enable rollback if needed.

memory_items.title: Null-Safe String Operations
memory_items.lesson: Check for None/null values before converting cells to strings or accessing string methods.
memory_items.generalization: Validate data types and existence before type conversions to prevent runtime errors during bulk operations.

memory_items.title: In-Memory Intermediate Storage
memory_items.lesson: Collect filtered or transformed data in memory structures before writing to output file.
memory_items.generalization: Use temporary data structures to stage changes, enabling batch writes and easier debugging of transformation logic.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Column Width Adjustment
memory_items.lesson: Recalculate column widths after data transformations based on the longest content in each column.
memory_items.generalization: Adapt layout properties to post-operation content sizes to maintain readability without manual intervention.

memory_items.title: Execution Metrics Logging
memory_items.lesson: Report counts of processed, kept, and modified rows/columns in operation summaries.
memory_items.generalization: Track quantitative metrics throughout transformations to enable verification and troubleshooting of data pipeline results.

memory_items.title: Metadata Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Maintain essential structural elements like headers and sheet names when reconstructing workbooks.
memory_items.generalization: Identify and explicitly preserve schema-defining elements (headers, named ranges, sheet identity) during regeneration operations.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Best Practices
- **Pre-Process Inspection**: Query sheet names, dimensions, and sample data before implementing transformation logic to validate assumptions.
- **File Safety**: Load input workbooks for reading, but write filtered outputs to separate files to protect source integrity.
- **Type-Safe Operations**: Check for None/null values explicitly before string conversions or method calls on cell contents.
- **Batch Processing**: Store filtered results in intermediate data structures before writing to enable validation and batch commits.
- **Layout Adaptation**: Automatically recalculate column widths based on post-transformation content lengths.
- **Metrics Tracking**: Log processed, kept, modified, and deleted counts to provide operation audit trails.
- **Schema Retention**: Preserve headers, sheet names, and other structural metadata when reconstructing workbooks after filtering.
